text.title: rust-backend Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for rust-backend. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Organise source into routes/, handlers/, services/, models/, db/, middleware/, error.rs, config.rs. Each layer depends only on the layer below it.
rule.rationale: Vertical consistency across services lets any engineer find files without reading a README. The compiler enforces the dependency direction.

rule.statement: Wrap all heavy fields in Arc. Pass State(state): State<AppState> to handlers — never individual sub-fields. Keep AppState Clone-cheap.
rule.rationale: Axum clones AppState per request. Wrapping Database, Config, etc. in Arc makes the clone O(1) and avoids accidental copies of connection pools.

rule.statement: Handlers return Result<impl IntoResponse, AppError>. Extract State, Path, Query, Json as typed parameters. Validate with the validator crate before calling the service.
rule.rationale: Axum calls IntoResponse on the Ok and Err variants. A typed AppError that implements IntoResponse keeps HTTP mapping out of service code.

rule.statement: Use tokio::try_join! to run independent DB queries concurrently rather than awaiting them sequentially.
rule.rationale: Sequential awaits for count + fetch add latency proportional to one RTT. try_join! runs both in parallel and fails fast on the first error.

rule.statement: Run CPU-intensive work (Argon2, bcrypt, large serialisation) via tokio::task::spawn_blocking. Never block the async executor with synchronous CPU loops.
rule.rationale: Async runtimes use a small thread pool. A blocking call on an async thread starves other tasks waiting for their poll.

rule.statement: Define a DatabaseTrait (or Repository trait) abstraction and mock it with mockall::mock!. Never mock a concrete struct whose methods use sqlx::query! macros — those embed file paths at compile time.
rule.rationale: sqlx::query! macros require DATABASE_URL at compile time. Mocking the concrete Database struct breaks when these macros are present. A trait abstraction separates the DB interface from the implementation.

rule.statement: Every variant of AppError must appear in the IntoResponse match arm. Adding a new variant without updating IntoResponse compiles but panics at runtime via the unreachable default.
rule.rationale: Rust does not force exhaustive matching when the fallback arm is present. A missing variant silently returns 500 with an empty body instead of the intended status code.

rule.statement: Map validator::ValidationErrors to per-field error arrays in the response. Never return the raw ValidationErrors debug string — it leaks field names in an uncontrolled format.
rule.rationale: The default Display for ValidationErrors is not RFC 7807 compatible. Clients receive an opaque string instead of a structured errors array.

rule.statement: Clone an sqlx::Pool to share across tasks — cloning is O(1) (Arc internally). Never call Pool::connect per request to "fix" Send issues.
rule.rationale: Pool::connect opens a new TCP connection. Calling it per request exhausts file descriptors under load. Pool clone shares the existing connection pool cheaply.

rule.statement: Layer middleware in this order, outer to inner: TraceLayer, request-id, CORS, auth, rate-limit, app router. Adding layers in wrong order silently misses authentication.
rule.rationale: Axum applies middleware from outermost to innermost. Auth middleware added after the route handler is never reached.

rule.statement: Run sqlx prepare and commit the .sqlx/ directory so CI builds without requiring DATABASE_URL at compile time.
rule.rationale: sqlx::query! macros verify SQL against a live DB at compile time. Without cached query data, offline builds and CI without a DB fail.

rule.statement: Every variant of AppError must have an IntoResponse arm. Use exhaustive matching on enum variants, not wildcard defaults.
rule.rationale: Missing IntoResponse arms compile (via default catch-all) but panic at runtime with 500 + empty body instead of the intended status.
